perturbation.original_text: Consolidated Artists agrees to cause Ogle to wear a patch and/or an embroidered non-patch bearing the Teardrop name and/or logo on the right chest, left sleeve, and back of his golf shirt and/or sweater (near the collar) and/or rain gear whenever he is playing competitive golf, participating in golf clinics and outings or otherwise engaging in golf promotional activities during the Contract Period and throughout the Contract Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Consolidated Artists agrees to use its best efforts to cause Ogle to wear a patch and/or an embroidered non-patch bearing the Teardrop name and/or logo on either the right chest or left sleeve, and potentially on the back of his golf shirt and/or sweater (near the collar) and/or rain gear only when he is playing in televised competitive golf events during the Contract Period and within the United States.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'agrees to cause' with 'agrees to use its best efforts', reducing the obligation's stringency. It also narrows the scope from all competitive golf, clinics, and outings to only televised competitive events within the United States and limits the specific places on the clothing and the fact that is not a must to be in all of them. This creates an in-text contradiction with Paragraph 3 (GRANT OF ENDORSEMENT RIGHTS) if that paragraph relies on the broader, more encompassing obligation initially defined in Paragraph 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, Consolidated Artists grants to TPC the exclusive right and license, within the Contract Territory and during the Contract Period, to use the Ogle Endorsement in connection with the manufacture, distribution, advertisement, promotion and sale of the Endorsed Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Paragraph 4 mandates Ogle to wear Teardrop endorsements in a wide range of golf activities and locations on his clothing. The changed text significantly narrows this obligation, limiting it to televised events within the US and providing flexibility on the placement of the logo. This contradicts Paragraph 3, which grants TPC the 'exclusive right and license' to use Ogle's endorsement. By reducing Ogle's required endorsement appearances, the changed text diminishes the value and scope of the 'exclusive right' granted to TPC, creating a direct conflict and thus, an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle royalty compensation of ten percent (10%) of the net sales of all Endorsed Products distributed or sold during the Contract Period in or to the Countries of Australia and New Zealand ("Royalty Compensation"). "Net sales" shall mean the gross invoice price billed to customers, less customary trade/quantity discounts, rebates and returns actually credited, but with no deductions of any kind.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle royalty compensation of ten percent (10%) of the net profits from sales of all Endorsed Products distributed or sold during the Contract Period in or to the Countries of Australia and New Zealand ("Royalty Compensation"). "Net profits" shall mean the gross income price billed to customers, less customary trade/quantity discounts, rebates and returns actually credited, with deductions of costs of advertising, sales and marketing.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the basis for calculating royalty compensation from 'net sales' to 'net profits' and redefine what it means. Including deducting costs of advertising, sales and marketing. This is a lower amount than net sales due to including costs of advertising, sales and marketing. This directly contradicts the original definition by changing the basis of calculation. This creates an internal contradiction if Paragraph 13 (ACCOUNTING FOR ROYALTY COMPENSATION) specifies accounting procedures based on 'net sales' as originally defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TPC agrees that it shall keep accurate and complete books and records showing all Endorsed Products manufactured, distributed and sold. Consolidated Artists and Advantage, at Consolidated Artists' expense, shall have the right during the Contract Period and until two (2) years after the termination of this Agreement to inspect and make copies of the books and records of TPC insofar as they relate to the computation of royalty payments due and owing to Consolidated Artists hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the basis of royalty calculation from 'net sales' to 'net profits', which includes deductions for advertising, sales, and marketing costs. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes royalty payments are based on 'net sales' and requires access to books and records related to the computation of royalty payments due and owing to Consolidated Artists. The change introduces a different accounting method (net profits vs. net sales) that would make the original accounting procedures and record-keeping requirements in the contradicted text incompatible. This represents an ambiguity that creates in-text contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: TPC acknowledges that time is of the essence in the payment of all compensation due Consolidated Artists hereunder. For the purposes of this Agreement, all payments not received within thirty (30) days of the date due shall be deemed "past due". Such past due payments shall bear interest at a rate of two percent (2%) per month OR the maximum rate permissible by law, whichever is less.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC acknowledges that prompt payment is appreciated for all compensation due Consolidated Artists hereunder. For the purposes of this Agreement, all payments not received within sixty (60) days of the date due shall be deemed "potentially subject to review". Such payments may bear interest at a rate to be mutually agreed upon at the time of delay, not to exceed the maximum rate permissible by law.
perturbation.explanation: The language regarding late payments has been significantly weakened. 'Time is of the essence' has been replaced with 'prompt payment is appreciated.' The definition of 'past due' has been altered from 30 days to 60 days, and the interest rate is no longer automatic but 'may' be applied at a rate to be agreed upon. This contradicts the implications within paragraph 21 (SPECIAL RIGHT OF TERMINATION BY CONSOLIDATED ARTISTS).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consolidated Artists shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to TPC in the event of the occurrence of any of the following contingencies: (a) If TPC is adjudicated as insolvent, declares bankruptcy or fails to continue its business of selling the Endorsed Product; or (b) If TPC fails to make payment to Consolidated Artists of any sums due pursuant to this Agreement within thirty (30) days following the date such payment is due hereunder, provided that TPC is notified in writing of such nonpayment by Consolidated Artists or Advantage and such payment is not made within ten (10) days following such notification. TPC agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to pay Consolidated Artists all compensation contemplated hereunder. Accordingly, Consolidated Artists shall not waive any of its rights at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment terms from a strict 30-day requirement with automatic interest to a more lenient 60-day period with interest subject to mutual agreement. This directly contradicts the original text's emphasis on 'time is of the essence' and a fixed interest rate. The contradicted text states that Consolidated Artists can terminate the agreement if TPC fails to make payment within 30 days of the due date. The changed text's extension of the 'past due' period to 60 days and the removal of automatic interest directly conflicts with Consolidated Artists' right to terminate for non-payment after 30 days, creating a clear contradiction and making the termination clause potentially unenforceable. The change significantly weakens the payment obligation, directly impacting the termination rights tied to that obligation. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction perturbation type because it introduces ambiguity in defining 'past due' and the consequences thereof.
